architecture.summary: Teriyaki is a neurosymbolic task planner that trains GPT-3 (via prompt-based in-context learning) to generate robot action plans compatible with PDDL. The LLM receives planning domain descriptions and current state as text context, then generates actions one-by-one in an autoregressive manner rather than end-to-end. This enables concurrent planning and execution while leveraging LLM generative capabilities to overcome symbolic planner limitations in dynamic, human-robot collaboration scenarios.

biblio.title: A framework for neurosymbolic robot action planning using large language models

claims.statement: Teriyaki can solve 95.5% of problems in a test data set of 1,000 samples
Teriyaki can produce plans up to 13.5% shorter than a traditional symbolic planner
Teriyaki can reduce average overall waiting times for a plan availability by up to 61.4%
LLMs' response time linearly scales with the combined length of the input and the output, instead of super-linearly as in the case of symbolic task planners

Teriyaki enables synthesis of a plan action-by-action instead of end-to-end, and makes each action available for execution as soon as it is generated instead of waiting for the whole plan to be available, which enables concurrent planning and execution
